summary: Distributability demands a three-way split the current scaffold conflates: (1) a PRODUCT repo (the OSS Knowledge Plane system — packages, published contracts, reference compose deployment, tight-DX layer), GitHub-canonical with an RFC-0016 reference-mode pull-mirror back into git-forge; (2) Alex's INSTANCE repo (the existing git-forge alex/knowledge-plane, repurposed: real endpoints, compose overrides, ADR-0029 fleet placement, secret references — never mirrored out); (3) the canonical KNOWLEDGE repos (the new Obsidian vault as its own private repo; now.md moves into the vault). The scaffold today mixes all three — vault/, now.md, LAN URLs, Alex-narration, and TODO(alex) endpoints sit inside the would-be-public monorepo — and its fleet/compose.yml + .mcp.json + README still assert the pre-ADR-0029-amendment story (MCP on the Mac, mcp-retrieval on pve, live-Obsidian-app dependency), so a fresh re-scaffold (one throwaway commit exists) is cheaper than surgery. The implied Node/npm-workspaces runtime should be reversed to a Python/uv workspace: every OCR tool RFC-0018 names is Python, the Python MCP SDK is mature, and Curio already owns the Rust/RSS side — the seam is file contracts, not FFI. The config surface a stranger needs is enumerable and small (vault path, optional Zotero creds+WebDAV, OpenAI-compatible LLM+embedding endpoints with Ollama-localhost defaults, pluggable vector/graph store with embedded default, MCP transport stdio-default/HTTP-optional, librarian cron + notification sink) and must ship as config.example.toml + .env.example + a `just demo` path. Licensing has one real landmine — PyMuPDF4LLM is AGPL and Marker is GPL-class, so the distributable OCR default must be Docling (MIT) with copyleft engines as optional subprocess-isolated extras under an Apache-2.0 (or MIT OR Apache-2.0) project license. "knowledge-plane" needs renaming before publication (generic, collides with the Clark-et-al networking term). Native Curio integration means the KP consumes frontmatter-v1/events-v1 as consumer #1 with cross-repo contract tests in CI, and RFC-0018's own RSS fetcher gets demoted to fallback — an explicit RFC amendment. ADR-0029 survives untouched: under the split, all its placement decisions become pure instance-side configuration, which validates rather than contradicts it.
facts: Scaffold contents (clone at scratchpad/knowledge-plane, single commit b5a8c36, origin ssh://git-forge@192.0.2.2/alex/knowledge-plane.git): README.md, package.json (npm workspaces packages/*, private:true, no deps/scripts), .gitignore (node_modules, .env, packages/index/data/, *.lance/, *.kuzu/), .mcp.json (all-TODO zotero stdio + obsidian HTTP entries), fleet/compose.yml (comments only, zero services), now.md (example thread only), vault/README.md (empty placeholder), packages/{ingest,index,librarian,mcp-retrieval}/README.md (prose only, no code), packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md (the one real distributable artifact).

Alex-instance material currently inside the product scaffold: README links LAN-only http://192.0.2.82/decisions/rfc-0018-knowledge-plane.html and narrates Alex's personal migration state; .mcp.json carries TODO(alex) comments; fleet/compose.yml names Alex's Mac and pve; now.md and vault/ are destined personal canonical content; librarian README names push-notifier, claude-agent/pi-agent, ADR-0029, and 'the brief in the hypervisor vault's inbox/'; index README names managed-postgres pgvector. No actual secrets exist in the repo or its one commit — only non-secret LAN IPs and narration — so no history scrubbing is required; a fresh public re-scaffold suffices.

House repo-boundary law (RFC-0014/ADR-0022, rfc/0014-management-plane-repo-boundary.md): app code lives in its own repo; the plane consumes external code only as SHA-pinned submodules under ansible/roles/**/files/; two orthogonal axes (A1 runtime management vs A2 code authorship). Under this law the KP instance repo can pin the product repo at a release SHA, matching how the plane already consumes projects-dashboard and proj-tooling.

House GitHub topology (RFC-0016, rfc/0016-github-repos-via-git-forge-mirroring.md): Mode A 'reference' = GitHub-canonical read-only pull-mirror into the git-forge mirrors/ org (8h interval, no token if public); Mode B 'fork' = git-forge-canonical with push-mirror to GitHub, external GitHub commits must be hand-reconciled (push-mirror never pulls; resync-github is fast-forward-only). GitHub PATs and the git-forge write token are host-only (/etc/projects-ctl/, Mac Keychain canonical); agent VMs and the dashboard CT never hold GitHub credentials. The estate also already push-mirrors alex/hypervisor out to GitHub (repo-third-copy precedent).

ADR-0029 (adr/0029-knowledge-plane-fleet-placement.md, amended 2026-07-02): MCP server processes (Zotero MCP, vault/retrieval MCP, ingress/TLS) run ON pve-net G11; the vault MCP reads a git-mirrored vault copy off disk, explicitly dropping the live-Obsidian-desktop-app dependency; Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced library copy (CT 206, 192.0.2.15, staged); OCR/embedding/indices/librarian on pve; pve-ha excluded; cross-host MCP-vs-index placement is why the vector store must be network-reachable (the pgvector lean in RFC-0018's open question).

The scaffold predates the ADR-0029 amendment: fleet/compose.yml says MCP servers 'run client-side on Alex's Mac' in Phase 1 and 'mcp-retrieval is deployed on pve' in Phase 3; .mcp.json instructs installing coddingtonbear's Local REST API plugin ('Requires the Obsidian desktop app running — not headless'); README's Phase 1 checklist repeats 'needs the live desktop app'. All three contradict the accepted placement.

Curio contracts the KP must consume natively (wf-results/designs.json core-plus-heads kp_seam + roadmap.json Phase 3): frontmatter-v1 (title, author, source URL, feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id UUIDv7, checksum = sha256 of normalized body, schema_version; named destinations — raw paths never cross IPC; in-vault .curio/manifest.json idempotency; atomic tmp+rename writes; deterministic slug-collision suffixes) and events-v1 (JSONL {schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted, article{curio_id,url,title,feed}, ts}; file sink with rotation + cursor checkpointing; webhook sink). Both are published, semver'd JSON Schema artifacts from curio-types with a written breaking-change policy, and Curio's roadmap mandates a schema-review gate before the first external consumer wires in — the KP is designated consumer #1. Curio has zero KP-specific code; KP integration is configuration only (point export at the vault inbox, point the librarian at the JSONL sink).

KP frontmatter schema (packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md) fields: title, source (zotero|rss|web|manual), zotero_key, url, authors, doi, date_added, tags, status (unprocessed|linked|promoted), checksum ('sha256:…' prefixed). It has NO schema_version field and diverges from Curio frontmatter-v1 (curio_id, feed, published, saved, schema_version; unprefixed checksum) — reconciliation must happen before Curio's schema-review gate, since a wrong identity key is a breaking change on both sides.

Curio's OSS-engineering precedent (wf-results/ossEng.json): dual MIT OR Apache-2.0 (AGPL argued down — local-first app, the real lock-in defense is the open vault/event schemas), DCO not CLA, trademark/domain clearance before launch (the Zengobi Curio conflict), GOVERNANCE.md 'personal-first, community-welcome', Conventional Commits + squash-merge + release-please changelog explicitly 'mirroring Alex's house style', GitHub Actions CI — i.e. Curio is GitHub-canonical, setting the sibling precedent for the KP product repo.

OCR tooling licensing (load-bearing for an OSS release): Docling is MIT (IBM); PyMuPDF4LLM depends on PyMuPDF which is AGPL-3.0/commercial dual-licensed; Marker is GPL-class with a commercial exception (verify current terms). RFC-0018 names all three in the OCR cascade; a permissively-licensed product repo can only ship Docling as the default engine, with the copyleft engines as optional user-installed extras invoked via subprocess isolation, licenses documented.

Config surface a stranger needs (complete enumeration): KP_VAULT_PATH (any markdown+YAML directory — Obsidian recommended viewer, never a dependency, which ADR-0029's git-mirror decision already guarantees architecturally); optional vault git remote (required only for librarian proposal branches); Zotero block optional (API key + user ID for zotero.org metadata, OR local zotero.sqlite path; WebDAV endpoint + basic-auth only for self-hosted attachments); Curio seam (export destination = vault inbox path; events sink path or webhook receiver + cursor state path); LLM endpoint (OpenAI-compatible base URL + key + model, default http://localhost:11434/v1 Ollama — Alex's instance overrides to llm-gateway 192.0.2.5:4242); embedding endpoint + model (bge-m3/nomic-embed-text/qwen3-embedding per index README) + chunking params, with the model recorded in index metadata so the index is self-describing; vector store driver (embedded default, pgvector optional); graph store (Kuzu embedded default per .gitignore hint); MCP transport (stdio default for .mcp.json single-user; Streamable HTTP + bind + port + bearer token for network deployment like Alex's G11); librarian cron schedules (maintenance loop + digest cadence), notification sink (pluggable: push-notifier/webhook/email/stdout — push-notifier is Alex's instance choice), now.md path, proposal-branch prefix; OCR engine selection + batch concurrency (CPU-only flags). Ship as config.example.toml + env overrides (KP_*) + .env.example; secrets only via env/file refs, never committed.

Language decision is genuinely open (packages are empty READMEs; package.json + RFC-0018's '@plane/*' naming merely imply Node) and the evidence points Python: PyMuPDF4LLM/Docling/Marker are all Python; the official Python MCP SDK (FastMCP) supports stdio + Streamable HTTP; LanceDB/Kuzu/pgvector all have first-class Python clients; embeddings arrive over HTTP (Ollama) so no in-process ML is needed; Curio already owns the Rust/RSS/desktop side and the integration seam is file contracts (JSONL + markdown), deliberately language-agnostic — no FFI ever. Node would still need Python subprocesses for OCR (two runtimes for strangers); Rust buys nothing at batch-job cadence. Recommended: single Python 3.12+ uv-workspace monorepo (pyproject.toml workspaces replace package.json), pydantic models emitting published JSON Schema artifacts (mirroring Curio's schemars pattern). The librarian remains harness-level (prompts/skills/scheduler), not a language commitment.

Alex's instance secret/endpoint homes (per _context conventions + secrets-registry doctrine): Mac Keychain canonical, secrets-manager CT 4 escrow, host-only token files; instance endpoints llm-gateway 192.0.2.5:4242, llm-observability 192.0.2.6:3000, managed-postgres 192.0.2.9 (supavisor :5432/:6543), push-notifier on G11, WebDAV CT 206 192.0.2.15, git-forge 192.0.2.2. None of these may appear in the product repo; all live in the instance repo's config + the registry.

Distributable deployment shape: fleet/compose.yml becomes a parameterized reference deployment with profiles (all-in-one single box vs split mcp/index), CPU-only images (no CUDA anywhere, matching the fleet constraint); Alex's ADR-0029 split placement is expressed as instance-repo compose overrides + config, not product-repo content. A `just demo` path (seeded sample vault + sample feed + Ollama defaults) is the distributability acceptance test: clone → uv sync → just demo must show ingest→index→search_hybrid end-to-end on one machine.

Tight-DX layer for the KP product repo (house doctrine applied): justfile (setup/up/down/ingest/reindex/mcp/librarian-once/digest-once/test/lint/schema-check/demo); hooks = ruff+format, mypy, conventional-commit msg gate, JSON Schema artifact regeneration, gitleaks secret-scan (public repo needs the leak gate the private plane doesn't); CI = GitHub Actions with lint/type/test matrix + a cross-repo contract job that pulls Curio's published frontmatter-v1/events-v1 schema artifacts and validates KP's parser fixtures against them (the enforcement mechanism for 'native, not bolt-on'); .claude/ = CLAUDE.md (canonical/derived rule, contracts, proposals-branch-only agent doctrine) + skills (/ingest-run, /reindex, /propose, /digest, /schema-bump) — shipped as part of the OSS product, with librarian prompts kept as plain markdown so non-Claude harnesses can consume them.
open_questions: Repo topology execution: recommend re-scaffolding the public product repo fresh under a new name on GitHub (canonical), pulling it into the estate via RFC-0016 Mode A reference mirror (read-only, mirrors/ org); repurpose the existing git-forge alex/knowledge-plane as the private instance repo (or create alex/kp-instance and retire it); the new Obsidian vault gets its own private repo, with now.md living inside the vault. Open: does Alex develop the product repo GitHub-side from the Mac (clean with community PRs; recommended) or via RFC-0016 Mode B fork from the agent VMs (inverts canonicality once external contributors merge on GitHub — the modes assume git-forge-centric dev and don't cleanly cover a community-canonical repo; would need an RFC-0016 note if chosen)?

Language/runtime: recommend reversing the scaffold's implied Node choice to a Python/uv workspace monorepo (rationale in facts; cost ~zero now — packages are empty, one commit). Needs Alex's sign-off since RFC-0018 prose says '@plane/*' and the scaffold shipped package.json.

Vector/graph store driver interface: recommend an embedded zero-infra default (LanceDB or sqlite-vec; Kuzu for graph) + a Postgres/pgvector driver, reframing RFC-0018's open LanceDB-vs-pgvector question from either/or to 'driver interface with embedded default; Alex's instance selects pgvector because ADR-0029 splits MCP (G11) from indices (pve)'. Confirm managed-postgres Postgres actually ships pgvector before Phase 3 (still unverified).

Full-text/BM25 ownership: RFC-0018 assumes 'free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch' — an Obsidian hard dependency that also contradicts ADR-0029's headless git-mirror posture. Recommend the system owns FTS (SQLite FTS5 or tantivy or pg tsvector, co-located with the chosen vector driver). Needs an explicit RFC-0018 amendment.

RSS ingestion delegation: with Curio native, recommend KP's ingestion plane consume Curio's frontmatter-v1 exports + events-v1 stream as the primary RSS path and demote RFC-0018's own RSS fetcher to an optional fallback for non-Curio users — an RFC-0018 amendment (Curio's design postdates the RFC). Open: does the product still ship a minimal built-in RSS fetcher for strangers without Curio, or is 'bring Curio or bring markdown' the honest scope?

License: recommend Apache-2.0 (or MIT OR Apache-2.0 for symmetry with Curio); AGPL rejected on Curio's own reasoning (local-first, contracts are the lock-in defense). Contingent on the OCR isolation decision: Docling (MIT) as default engine, PyMuPDF4LLM (AGPL) and Marker (GPL-class) as optional subprocess-isolated extras — verify both licenses' current terms before release.

Name: 'knowledge-plane' must be renamed before publication (generic; collides with the established Clark-et-al networking-research term; unbrandable). Criteria: PyPI + GitHub org + domain free, no trademark conflict, pairs with Curio (cabinet-of-curiosities theme). Alex's call; run the same trademark/domain clearance Curio's plan mandates.

Librarian portability: Alex's instance runs it as a Claude Code agent VM (claude-agent/pi-agent pattern), but the OSS system must not require a Claude subscription. Recommend shipping the librarian as prompts + skills + scheduler with two harness adapters (Claude Code reference implementation first; a provider-neutral OpenAI-compatible agent loop second). Open: how much v1 investment the neutral adapter gets.

Frontmatter contract reconciliation: KP schema vs Curio frontmatter-v1 diverge (no schema_version in KP; checksum format 'sha256:…' vs raw; curio_id/feed/published/saved vs zotero_key/doi/date_added/status). Decide the joint identity key and field mapping at Curio's mandatory schema-review gate BEFORE any consumer code exists — recommend KP adopts schema_version + accepts frontmatter-v1 as-is and layers its lifecycle fields (status) as KP-namespaced additions.

MCP server sourcing: RFC-0018 rejects building Zotero/Obsidian MCP servers from scratch (names 54yyyu/zotero-mcp-server, ZotPilot, coddingtonbear, cyanheads as candidates), but the coddingtonbear path is now non-canonical (live-app dependency) and a distributable system wiring third-party MCP servers needs pinned, license-compatible choices. Open: which Zotero MCP server the product blesses, and whether the vault MCP (headless, reads files) is thin enough to just build in-repo alongside mcp-retrieval (recommended — it is the distributable, Obsidian-free answer).
stale_or_wrong: fleet/compose.yml (scaffold) asserts Phase 1 MCP servers 'run client-side on Alex's Mac' and Phase 3 'mcp-retrieval is deployed on pve' — both contradicted by ADR-0029's accepted 2026-07-02 amendment placing all MCP server processes on pve-net G11.

.mcp.json (scaffold) instructs installing the Obsidian Local REST API plugin requiring the live desktop app — ADR-0029 explicitly dropped that dependency (vault MCP reads a git-mirrored copy off disk); following the scaffold's instruction would also create the Obsidian hard dependency the new directive forbids.

README.md (scaffold) Phase 1 checklist repeats 'Obsidian MCP server (needs the live desktop app, not headless — RFC-0018's open-questions note)' — that open question was resolved 2026-07-02; the scaffold text was never swept after the amendment. Also links LAN-only 192.0.2.82 docs as the design record — unusable in a public repo; the architecture needs distilling into in-repo public docs.

RFC-0018's index plane claims full-text/BM25 is 'free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch' — inconsistent with ADR-0029's own headless git-mirror decision and non-distributable (proprietary-app dependency); FTS must be system-owned. Amendment-worthy.

RFC-0018's ingestion plane specifies a KP-built RSS fetcher — underdesigned given Curio (designed one day later) is the designated native reading surface with published export/events contracts; RSS ingestion should be contract consumption, with any built-in fetcher demoted to fallback. Amendment-worthy.

package.json npm-workspaces + RFC-0018's '@plane/*' naming imply a Node monorepo, but every OCR tool the RFC itself names is Python — the scaffold's implied runtime contradicts its own tooling gravity; the language decision is open and should be settled as Python before any package gains code.

vault/ and now.md live inside the product monorepo — canonical personal content inside distributable derived machinery violates both distributability and the clean canonical/derived separation (different lifecycle/backup postures); both must move to the vault/instance side, with now.md becoming an in-vault convention at a configurable path.

packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md is presented as 'locked' (README checkbox checked) but lacks schema_version and is unreconciled with Curio frontmatter-v1 — locking it before Curio's schema-review gate would bake in a breaking identity-key divergence with the system's own designated consumer-#1 relationship.

RFC-0018/ADR-0029 frame the KP as purely 'personal, self-hosted' — the distributable-OSS-product requirement is a new structural fact neither record covers (product/instance repo split, GitHub canonicality, licensing, naming, pluggable-stack requirement); needs a new RFC or an explicit RFC-0018 amendment rather than silent scope creep. ADR-0029 itself survives unamended: under the product/instance split all its placement decisions become instance-side configuration, which the split validates.
